page: Microsoft Azure D192s_v6 is a Dsv6-family virtual machine configured with 192 dedicated vCPUs on an x86_64 Intel processor architecture. Running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance provides 768.0 GB of system memory, maintaining a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per virtual core.

This server type does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, meaning all storage requirements must be met through external Premium Storage options. Additionally, there are zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses included with this configuration. The lack of local NVMe storage and hardware acceleration limits its direct applicability for workloads requiring local scratch space or GPU-driven parallel processing.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the D192s_v6 offers high resource density for large-scale general-purpose workloads. It is designed for enterprise applications, large-scale web serving, and distributed database nodes that require substantial CPU and memory capacity but rely on network-attached storage.
description: Microsoft Azure D192s_v6 is a Dsv6-family virtual machine featuring 192 dedicated vCPUs on an x86_64 Intel processor architecture. Operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, it delivers 768.0 GB of system memory, establishing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core ratio. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, requiring reliance on external Premium Storage for data persistence. The high core count and memory capacity make it suitable for large-scale general-purpose workloads, enterprise application hosting, and distributed database nodes that do not require local disk storage or hardware acceleration.

tagline: A high-density, general-purpose virtual machine featuring one hundred ninety-two dedicated x86_64 cores and seven hundred sixty-eight gigabytes of system memory.
